75935.body: #outlook a { padding:0; } body { margin:0;padding:0;-webkit-text-size-adjust:100%;-ms-text-size-adjust:100%; } table, td { border-collapse:collapse;mso-table-lspace:0pt;mso-table-rspace:0pt; } img { border:0;height:auto;line-height:100%; outline:none;text-decoration:none;-ms-interpolation-mode:bicubic; } p { display:block;margin:13px 0; } 96 .mj-outlook-group-fix { width:100% !important; } @import url(https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Roboto:300,400,500,700); @import url(https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Ubuntu:300,400,500,700); @media only screen and (min-width:480px) { .mj-column-per-100 { width:100% !important; max-width: 100%; } .mj-column-per-50 { width:50% !important; max-width: 50%; } } .moz-text-html .mj-column-per-100 { width:100% !important; max-width: 100%; } .moz-text-html .mj-column-per-50 { width:50% !important; max-width: 50%; } @media only screen and (max-width:480px) { table.mj-full-width-mobile { width: 100% !important; } td.mj-full-width-mobile { width: auto !important; } } :root { color-scheme: light dark; supported-color-schemes: light dark; } .main-container { background-color: #fff !important; padding-bottom: 20px; } .main-title { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Trebuchet MS', Roboto, Ubuntu, sans-serif; font-size: 36px; line-height: 44px; font-weight: 590; color: #000 !important; } .regular-text, .regular-text * { color: #000 !important; } [data-ogsc] .main-container { background-color: #fff !important; } [data-ogsc] .square-btn a { background-color: #010101 !important; color: #F5F5F5 !important; } [data-ogsc] .main-title, .regular-text, .regular-text * { color: #000 !important; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark ) { .main-container { background-color: #000 !important; } .square-btn a { background-color: #F5F5F5 !important; color: #010101 !important; } .main-title, .regular-text, .regular-text * { color: #FEFEFE !important; } } @media only screen and (max-width: 500px) { div.main-container > table > tbody > tr > td { padding-left: 20px !important; padding-right: 20px !important; } }       LuxAlgo   на которого вы подписаны, опубликовал(а) новый скрипт (indicator)   Order Flow 3D Delta Profile [LuxAlgo] The Order Flow 3D Delta Profile indicator provides a spatial visualization of volume delta distribution across a specified lookback period, using 3D extrusion to represent market depth and intensity⁠. 🔶 USAGE The indicator projects a 3D volume profile onto the chart, allowing traders to identify price levels with significant buying or selling pressure⁠. Unlike traditional 2D profiles, this tool uses depth and perspective to highlight the relationship between price, volume, and time&#828

75932.subject: Everything Down, Except Oil and Solana
75932.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Hi all, happy Tuesday! Today, we break down the broader risk-off move in global markets, why select Solana ecosystem tokens are outperforming, and Jito’s launch of JTX, its new institutional-grade trading interface.

Let’s get into it.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

BTC closed Monday down 2.5%, underperforming amid a broader risk-off move that also pushed equities and gold lower. Oil, meanwhile, rallied as much as 8% as clashes around the Strait of Hormuz raised concerns over global energy supplies.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/5f3c62b6-a067-49e3-9b27-443541608c29/image.png?t=1784032421)
Caption: 

Macro conditions could still create further headwinds. Fed Governor Christopher Waller said near-term rate hikes could return to the table if inflation comes in hot. However, this morning’s cooler-than-expected CPI release eased those concerns, with both headline and core inflation declining in June. CME FedWatch now assigns roughly a 17% probability to a hike at the next meeting, down from nearly 42% yesterday.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/2c9438c4-7566-4c19-bb1c-9560a55efc68/image.png?t=1784035316)
Caption: 

Despite the market uncertainty, the Solana Ecosystem index showed notable relative strength, led by META (+10%), JTO (+5.5%), and CLOUD (+3.4%).

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dfc518d0-046b-4a37-92cb-499dc04d696c/image.png?t=1784032421)
Caption: 

META’s rally follows continued demand for MetaDAO launches. Two weeks after Laso Finance attracted more than $25 million in commitments before capping its sale at $1 million, Credible Finance drew $6 million within two minutes of opening yesterday and had reached $21M as of this morning. MetaDAO remains crypto’s most credible attempt at realizing the Internet Capital Markets vision through permissionless capital formation and decision markets.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/409219db-df77-4cf6-83a7-25d5d1fbc45f/image.png?t=1784032421)
Caption: 

JTO also outperformed ahead of today’s JTX exchange launch, which Toma covers below. Separately, the Jito Foundation proposed JIP-38, which would formally establish Jito as a token-centric network wher

75929.subject: Prompt Engineering Is Becoming the Smallest Part of AI (Loop Engineering🚀)
75929.body:
AI Frontier

Автор: Steve Nouri


Автор: Steve Nouri

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/prompt-engineering-becoming-smallest-part-ai-loop-steve-nouri-7gyac?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B784o%2FByYQce2AbIkdnQd2w%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3AsTAbGkYgJck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrknur2a~v1-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrknur2a-v1
  
Prompt Engineering Is Becoming the Smallest Part of AI Half your feed is suddenly saying the same thing: Stop prompting agents. Start engineering loops. Boris Cherny, who leads Claude Code at Anthropic, put it clearly: “I do not prompt Claude anymore. I have loops running. My job is to write loops.” That is a bigger shift than it sounds. Most people still use AI like this: Prompt → Answer → Review → Fix → Prompt again The AI generates the output, but the human still plans, checks and corrects everything. The human is still the loop. Loop engineering changes that. Instead of asking an agent for one answer, you build a system that keeps working until the result passes a real test. Goal → Execute → Check → Fix → Repeat Prompting gives an agent an instruction. Loop engineering gives it a job. The loop itself is easy Most agents run some version of this: The model reads the context. It calls a tool. The result returns to the model. The cycle continues. Nobody is winning because they wrote a better while loop. The advantage comes from everything around it: What context does the model receive? Which tools can it access? How are errors handled? What proves the work is finished? When should the system stop? That is where the real engineering now lives. Still wondering why your agent keeps forgetting what it was doing? The LLM is a tiny part of the system. Everyone focuses on it, but the stuff around it is where everything actually falls apart at scale. Memory, security, tracking what happened, recovery from crashes – that's where the engineering lives. MongoDB walks through 6 components that separate a cool demo from what you can rely on (plus, the infrastructure layer underneath that lets you run dozens of agents across your organization smoothly). If you've heard “harness engineering” floating around but weren't sure what the hype was about, this explains it clearly. Genuinely worth reading. 👉 The full article The AI stack keeps expanding First came prompt engineering: improving the instructions. Then context engineering: controlling everything the model sees. Then harness engineering: building the tools, memory, permissions and error handling around the model. Now we have loop engineering: connecting those pieces into a system that can work toward a verified outcome. The prompt still matters. It is simply one component inside a much larger mac

75918.subject: predbežná kalkulácia
75918.body:
Dobrý deň, 

posielam Vám predbežnú kalkuláciu, prosím o kontrolu či je všetko v poriadku.


Prosím do nedele 19.7., najneskôr do pondelka 20.7. mi pošlite zasadací poriadok vo worde alebo v exceli.

Ďakujem pekne.






S pozdravom / Best regards

Bc. Lucia Ortutová
Event manager/wedding coordinator
